architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, no MLP, raw softmax(QK^T/τ) — minimal delta from base_model.py that exactly solves the one-hot lookup goal.

baseline_comparison.note: Plots the analytic uniform 1/L reference but runs no measured strawman ablation (e.g. no-exp / no-temperature) that fails where the method succeeds.

faithfulness.note: Mechanism is the model so the measured pattern is the circuit, but it's purely synthetic with no ablation and the README doesn't explicitly frame faithfulness or propose a causal check.

operating_range.note: Holds across the full L=16→256 sweep (target_attention 0.999→0.989, robustness 0.989) with graceful entropy growth.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-built, zero learned parameters — the analytic dot-product circuit reproduces the target behaviour directly.

visual_judgement.note: Clean 4-panel plot (target, peak vs target, entropy, output cosine) with baselines on a shared log-length axis; legible though slightly more panels than strictly needed.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each curve to the claim and justifies log-length axis and uniform baseline; clearly connects chart to what the goal asks.

notes: A clean, correct hand-built dot-product attention head that achieves near-perfect one-hot lookup (canonical 0.997, robustness 0.989) across the full length sweep with strong hardcoded-circuit evidence and a legible visualisation. It is held back on the priority items: the baseline is only the analytic 1/L reference rather than a measured failing strawman, and faithfulness rests on the synthetic construction with no ablation or proposed causal check. Solid demonstration of the primitive, but borderline because the dominant rubric items (baseline/faithfulness) are at tier 3.
